title: Vantara's EU Troubles Cast Shadow Over Hanseong Deal
body: The regulatory problems mounting for Vantara AI in Europe are beginning to complicate Hanseong Group's licensing arrangement with the Swedish company, according to investors and analysts tracking both firms. Hanseong's shares fell 1.8 percent Thursday following confirmation of a preliminary GDPR violation finding against Vantara, its new technology partner. Ji-Yeon Kwon, Hanseong's CFO and the executive who led the deal, faces questions about whether adequate due diligence was performed before the agreement was signed. One institutional investor told Asian Investor Review that Kwon's team was aware of the Irish inquiry before the deal closed but assessed the risk as manageable. Another person familiar with the matter said Hanseong's legal team had requested additional representations from Vantara about its data practices that were ultimately not provided before signing. Hanseong issued a brief statement saying it has "full confidence" in the licensing arrangement and that Vantara's data infrastructure plans, including the move to European servers, address the underlying concerns. Bergström, for his part, did not appear at this week's scheduled investor call.
